source_note: Fetched from ArduPilot machine-readable apm.pdef.json and compacted to the skill's investigation-focused parameter subset.
caveat: Parameter metadata may not exactly match the firmware that produced the log. Latest-source metadata may include unreleased, renamed, or removed parameters. Use as explanatory context, not proof of firmware-specific behaviour or automatic parameter-change advice.

parameters.name: ARMING_CHECK
parameters.description: Controls which pre-arm safety checks are required before arming on firmware that uses ARMING_CHECK. Newer metadata may expose related arming check parameters instead.
parameters.source_note: Fallback compact metadata because this exact parameter was not present in the fetched latest-source metadata.

parameters.name: ATC_ANG_YAW_P
parameters.display_name: Yaw axis angle controller P gain
parameters.description: Yaw axis angle controller P gain.  Converts the error between the desired yaw angle and actual angle to a desired yaw rate

parameters.name: ATC_RATE_Y_MAX
parameters.description: Maximum angular velocity in yaw axis

parameters.name: ATC_RAT_YAW_D
parameters.display_name: Yaw axis rate controller D gain
parameters.description: Yaw axis rate controller D gain.  Compensates for short-term change in desired yaw rate vs actual yaw rate

parameters.name: ATC_RAT_YAW_FF
parameters.display_name: Yaw axis rate controller feed forward
parameters.description: Yaw axis rate controller feed forward

parameters.name: ATC_RAT_YAW_I
parameters.display_name: Yaw axis rate controller I gain
parameters.description: Yaw axis rate controller I gain.  Corrects long-term difference in desired yaw rate vs actual yaw rate

parameters.name: ATC_RAT_YAW_P
parameters.display_name: Yaw axis rate controller P gain
parameters.description: Yaw axis rate controller P gain.  Corrects in proportion to the difference between the desired yaw rate vs actual yaw rate

parameters.name: BATT_*
parameters.description: Wildcard metadata for BATT_ parameter family; exact meaning depends on the specific parameter.

parameters.name: BATT_AMP_OFFSET
parameters.description: Voltage offset at zero current on current sensor

parameters.name: BATT_AMP_PERVLT
parameters.description: Number of amps that a 1V reading on the current sensor corresponds to.

parameters.name: BATT_ANX_CANDRV
parameters.description: Set ANX CAN driver

parameters.name: BATT_ANX_ENABLE
parameters.description: Enable ANX battery support

parameters.name: BATT_ANX_INDEX
parameters.description: ANX CAN battery index

parameters.name: BATT_ANX_OPTIONS
parameters.description: ANX CAN battery options

parameters.name: BATT_ARM_MAH
parameters.description: Battery capacity remaining which is required to arm the aircraft. Set to 0 to allow arming at any capacity. Note that execept for smart batteries rebooting the vehicle will always reset the remaining capacity estimate, which can lead to this check not providing sufficent protection, it is recommended to always use this in conjunction with the BATT_ARM_VOLT parameter.

parameters.name: BATT_ARM_VOLT
parameters.description: Battery voltage level which is required to arm the aircraft. Set to 0 to allow arming at any voltage.

parameters.name: BATT_CAPACITY
parameters.description: Capacity of the battery in mAh when full

parameters.name: BATT_CHANNEL
parameters.description: INA3221 channel to return data for

parameters.name: BATT_CRT_MAH
parameters.description: Battery capacity at which the critical battery failsafe is triggered. Set to 0 to disable battery remaining failsafe. If the battery capacity drops below this level the vehicle will perform the failsafe specified by the BATT_FS_CRT_ACT parameter.

parameters.name: BATT_CRT_VOLT
parameters.description: Battery voltage that triggers a critical battery failsafe. Set to 0 to disable. If the battery voltage drops below this voltage continuously for more then the period specified by the BATT_LOW_TIMER parameter then the vehicle will perform the failsafe specified by the BATT_FS_CRT_ACT parameter.

parameters.name: BATT_CURR_MULT
parameters.description: Multiplier applied to all current related reports to allow for adjustment if no UAVCAN param access or current splitting applications

parameters.name: BATT_CURR_PIN
parameters.description: Sets the analog input pin that should be used for Current monitoring on AD7091R5.

parameters.name: BATT_ESC_INDEX
parameters.display_name: ESC Telemetry Index to write to
parameters.description: ESC Telemetry Index to write voltage, current, consumption and temperature data to. Use 0 to disable.

parameters.name: BATT_ESC_MASK
parameters.description: If 0 all connected ESCs will be used. If non-zero, only those selected in will be used.

parameters.name: BATT_FL_FF
parameters.description: First order polynomial fit term

parameters.name: BATT_FL_FLTR
parameters.description: Filter frequency in Hertz where a low pass filter is used. This is used to filter out tank slosh from the fuel level reading. A value of -1 disables the filter and unfiltered voltage is used to determine the fuel level. The suggested values at in the range of 0.2 Hz to 0.5 Hz.

parameters.name: BATT_FL_FS
parameters.description: Second order polynomial fit term

parameters.name: BATT_FL_FT
parameters.description: Third order polynomial fit term

parameters.name: BATT_FL_OFF
parameters.description: Offset polynomial fit term

parameters.name: BATT_FL_PIN
parameters.description: Analog input pin that fuel level sensor is connected to.Analog Airspeed or RSSI ports can be used for Analog input( some autopilots provide others also). Values for some autopilots are given as examples. Search wiki for "Analog pins".

parameters.name: BATT_FL_VLT_MIN
parameters.description: The voltage seen on the analog pin when the fuel tank is empty. Note: For this type of battery monitor, the voltage seen by the analog pin is displayed as battery voltage on a GCS.

parameters.name: BATT_FL_V_MULT
parameters.description: Voltage multiplier to determine what the full tank voltage reading is. This is calculated as 1 / (Voltage_Full - Voltage_Empty) Note: For this type of battery monitor, the voltage seen by the analog pin is displayed as battery voltage on a GCS.

parameters.name: BATT_FS_CRT_ACT
parameters.description: What action the vehicle should perform if it hits a critical battery failsafe

parameters.name: BATT_FS_LOW_ACT
parameters.description: What action the vehicle should perform if it hits a low battery failsafe

parameters.name: BATT_FS_VOLTSRC
parameters.description: Voltage type used for detection of low voltage event

parameters.name: BATT_I2C_ADDR
parameters.description: Battery monitor I2C address. If this is zero then probe list of supported addresses

parameters.name: BATT_I2C_BUS
parameters.description: Battery monitor I2C bus number

parameters.name: BATT_IDX
parameters.description: Ensure this battery is configured with `BATT*_MONITOR=29`.

parameters.name: BATT_LOW_MAH
parameters.description: Battery capacity at which the low battery failsafe is triggered. Set to 0 to disable battery remaining failsafe. If the battery capacity drops below this level the vehicle will perform the failsafe specified by the BATT_FS_LOW_ACT parameter.

parameters.name: BATT_LOW_TIMER
parameters.description: This is the timeout in seconds before a low voltage event will be triggered. For aircraft with low C batteries it may be necessary to raise this in order to cope with low voltage on long takeoffs. A value of zero disables low voltage errors.

parameters.name: BATT_LOW_VOLT
parameters.description: Battery voltage that triggers a low battery failsafe. Set to 0 to disable. If the battery voltage drops below this voltage continuously for more then the period specified by the BATT_LOW_TIMER parameter then the vehicle will perform the failsafe specified by the BATT_FS_LOW_ACT parameter.

parameters.name: BATT_MAX_AMPS
parameters.description: This controls the maximum current the INA239 sensor will work with.

parameters.name: BATT_MAX_VOLT
parameters.description: Maximum voltage of battery. Provides scaling of current versus voltage

parameters.name: BATT_MONITOR
parameters.description: Controls enabling monitoring of the battery's voltage and current

parameters.name: BATT_OPTIONS
parameters.description: This sets options to change the behaviour of the battery monitor

bitmask.1: MPPT reports input voltage and current
bitmask.10: Allow DroneCAN dynamic node update on hot-swap
bitmask.6: Send resistance compensated voltage to GCS
bitmask.7: Allow DroneCAN InfoAux to be from a different CAN node
bitmask.8: Battery is for internal autopilot use only
bitmask.9: Sum monitor measures minimum voltage instead of average

parameters.name: BATT_SERIAL_NUM
parameters.description: Battery serial number, automatically filled in for SMBus batteries, otherwise will be -1. With DroneCan it is the battery_id.

parameters.name: BATT_SHUNT
parameters.description: This sets the shunt resistor used in the device

parameters.name: BATT_SOC1_C1
parameters.description: Battery estimator coefficient1

parameters.name: BATT_SOC1_C2
parameters.description: Battery estimator coefficient2

parameters.name: BATT_SOC1_C3
parameters.description: Battery estimator coefficient3

parameters.name: BATT_SOC1_C4
parameters.description: Battery estimator coefficient4

parameters.name: BATT_SOC1_IDX
parameters.description: Battery estimator index

parameters.name: BATT_SOC1_NCELL
parameters.description: Battery estimator cell count

parameters.name: BATT_SOC2_C1
parameters.description: Battery estimator coefficient1

parameters.name: BATT_SOC2_C2
parameters.description: Battery estimator coefficient2

parameters.name: BATT_SOC2_C3
parameters.description: Battery estimator coefficient3

parameters.name: BATT_SOC2_C4
parameters.description: Battery estimator coefficient4

parameters.name: BATT_SOC2_IDX
parameters.description: Battery estimator index

parameters.name: BATT_SOC2_NCELL
parameters.description: Battery estimator cell count

parameters.name: BATT_SOC3_C1
parameters.description: Battery estimator coefficient1

parameters.name: BATT_SOC3_C2
parameters.description: Battery estimator coefficient2

parameters.name: BATT_SOC3_C3
parameters.description: Battery estimator coefficient3

parameters.name: BATT_SOC3_C4
parameters.description: Battery estimator coefficient4

parameters.name: BATT_SOC3_IDX
parameters.description: Battery estimator index

parameters.name: BATT_SOC3_NCELL
parameters.description: Battery estimator cell count

parameters.name: BATT_SOC4_C1
parameters.description: Battery estimator coefficient1

parameters.name: BATT_SOC4_C2
parameters.description: Battery estimator coefficient2

parameters.name: BATT_SOC4_C3
parameters.description: Battery estimator coefficient3

parameters.name: BATT_SOC4_C4
parameters.description: Battery estimator coefficient4

parameters.name: BATT_SOC4_IDX
parameters.description: Battery estimator index

parameters.name: BATT_SOC4_NCELL
parameters.description: Battery estimator cell count

parameters.name: BATT_SOC_COUNT
parameters.description: Number of battery SOC estimators

parameters.name: BATT_SUM_MASK
parameters.description: 0: sum of remaining battery monitors, If none 0 sum of specified monitors. Current will be summed and voltages averaged.

parameters.name: BATT_VLT_OFFSET
parameters.description: Voltage offset on voltage pin. This allows for an offset due to a diode. This voltage is subtracted before the scaling is applied

parameters.name: BATT_VOLT_MULT
parameters.description: Used to convert the voltage of the voltage sensing pin (BATT_VOLT_PIN) to the actual battery's voltage (pin_voltage * VOLT_MULT).

parameters.name: BATT_VOLT_PIN
parameters.display_name: Battery Voltage sensing pin on the AD7091R5 Ic
parameters.description: Sets the analog input pin that should be used for voltage monitoring on AD7091R5.

parameters.name: COMPASS_*
parameters.description: Wildcard metadata for COMPASS_ parameter family; exact meaning depends on the specific parameter.

parameters.name: COMPASS_AUTODEC
parameters.description: Enable or disable the automatic calculation of the declination based on gps location

parameters.name: COMPASS_AUTO_ROT
parameters.description: When enabled this will automatically check the orientation of compasses on successful completion of compass calibration. If set to 2 then external compasses will have their orientation automatically corrected.

values.3: use same tolerance to auto rotate 45 deg rotations

parameters.name: COMPASS_CAL_FIT
parameters.description: This controls the fitness level required for a successful compass calibration. A lower value makes for a stricter fit (less likely to pass). This is the value used for the primary magnetometer. Other magnetometers get double the value.

parameters.name: COMPASS_CUS_PIT
parameters.description: Compass mounting position pitch offset. Positive values = pitch up, negative values = pitch down. This parameter is only used when COMPASS_ORIENT/2/3 is set to CUSTOM.

parameters.name: COMPASS_CUS_ROLL
parameters.description: Compass mounting position roll offset. Positive values = roll right, negative values = roll left. This parameter is only used when COMPASS_ORIENT/2/3 is set to CUSTOM.

parameters.name: COMPASS_CUS_YAW
parameters.description: Compass mounting position yaw offset. Positive values = yaw right, negative values = yaw left. This parameter is only used when COMPASS_ORIENT/2/3 is set to CUSTOM.

parameters.name: COMPASS_DEC
parameters.description: An angle to compensate between the true north and magnetic north

parameters.name: COMPASS_DEV_ID
parameters.description: Compass device id.  Automatically detected, do not set manually

parameters.name: COMPASS_DEV_ID2
parameters.description: Second compass's device id.  Automatically detected, do not set manually

parameters.name: COMPASS_DEV_ID3
parameters.description: Third compass's device id.  Automatically detected, do not set manually

parameters.name: COMPASS_DEV_ID4
parameters.description: Extra 4th compass's device id.  Automatically detected, do not set manually

parameters.name: COMPASS_DEV_ID5
parameters.description: Extra 5th compass's device id.  Automatically detected, do not set manually

parameters.name: COMPASS_DEV_ID6
parameters.description: Extra 6th compass's device id.  Automatically detected, do not set manually

parameters.name: COMPASS_DEV_ID7
parameters.description: Extra 7th compass's device id.  Automatically detected, do not set manually

parameters.name: COMPASS_DEV_ID8
parameters.description: Extra 8th compass's device id.  Automatically detected, do not set manually

parameters.name: COMPASS_DIA2_X
parameters.description: DIA_X in the compass2 soft-iron calibration matrix: [[DIA_X, ODI_X, ODI_Y], [ODI_X, DIA_Y, ODI_Z], [ODI_Y, ODI_Z, DIA_Z]]

parameters.name: COMPASS_DIA2_Y
parameters.description: DIA_Y in the compass2 soft-iron calibration matrix: [[DIA_X, ODI_X, ODI_Y], [ODI_X, DIA_Y, ODI_Z], [ODI_Y, ODI_Z, DIA_Z]]

parameters.name: COMPASS_DIA2_Z
parameters.description: DIA_Z in the compass2 soft-iron calibration matrix: [[DIA_X, ODI_X, ODI_Y], [ODI_X, DIA_Y, ODI_Z], [ODI_Y, ODI_Z, DIA_Z]]

parameters.name: COMPASS_DIA3_X
parameters.description: DIA_X in the compass3 soft-iron calibration matrix: [[DIA_X, ODI_X, ODI_Y], [ODI_X, DIA_Y, ODI_Z], [ODI_Y, ODI_Z, DIA_Z]]

parameters.name: COMPASS_DIA3_Y
parameters.description: DIA_Y in the compass3 soft-iron calibration matrix: [[DIA_X, ODI_X, ODI_Y], [ODI_X, DIA_Y, ODI_Z], [ODI_Y, ODI_Z, DIA_Z]]

parameters.name: COMPASS_DIA3_Z
parameters.description: DIA_Z in the compass3 soft-iron calibration matrix: [[DIA_X, ODI_X, ODI_Y], [ODI_X, DIA_Y, ODI_Z], [ODI_Y, ODI_Z, DIA_Z]]

parameters.name: COMPASS_DIA_X
parameters.description: DIA_X in the compass soft-iron calibration matrix: [[DIA_X, ODI_X, ODI_Y], [ODI_X, DIA_Y, ODI_Z], [ODI_Y, ODI_Z, DIA_Z]]

parameters.name: COMPASS_DIA_Y
parameters.description: DIA_Y in the compass soft-iron calibration matrix: [[DIA_X, ODI_X, ODI_Y], [ODI_X, DIA_Y, ODI_Z], [ODI_Y, ODI_Z, DIA_Z]]

parameters.name: COMPASS_DIA_Z
parameters.description: DIA_Z in the compass soft-iron calibration matrix: [[DIA_X, ODI_X, ODI_Y], [ODI_X, DIA_Y, ODI_Z], [ODI_Y, ODI_Z, DIA_Z]]

parameters.name: COMPASS_DISBLMSK
parameters.description: This is a bitmask of driver types to disable. If a driver type is set in this mask then that driver will not try to find a sensor at startup

parameters.name: COMPASS_ENABLE
parameters.description: Setting this to Enabled(1) will enable the compass. Setting this to Disabled(0) will disable the compass. Note that this is separate from COMPASS_USE. This will enable the low level senor, and will enable logging of magnetometer data. To use the compass for navigation you must also set COMPASS_USE to 1.

parameters.name: COMPASS_EXTERN2
parameters.display_name: Compass2 is attached via an external cable
parameters.description: Configure second compass so it is attached externally. This is auto-detected on most boards. If set to 0 or 1 then auto-detection by bus connection can override the value. If set to 2 then auto-detection will be disabled.

parameters.name: COMPASS_EXTERN3
parameters.display_name: Compass3 is attached via an external cable
parameters.description: Configure third compass so it is attached externally. This is auto-detected on most boards. If set to 0 or 1 then auto-detection by bus connection can override the value. If set to 2 then auto-detection will be disabled.

parameters.name: COMPASS_EXTERNAL
parameters.display_name: Compass is attached via an external cable
parameters.description: Configure compass so it is attached externally. This is auto-detected on most boards. Set to 1 if the compass is externally connected. When externally connected the COMPASS_ORIENT option operates independently of the AHRS_ORIENTATION board orientation option. If set to 0 or 1 then auto-detection by bus connection can override the value. If set to 2 then auto-detection will be disabled.

parameters.name: COMPASS_FLTR_RNG
parameters.display_name: Range in which sample is accepted
parameters.description: This sets the range around the average value that new samples must be within to be accepted. This can help reduce the impact of noise on sensors that are on long I2C cables. The value is a percentage from the average value. A value of zero disables this filter.

parameters.name: COMPASS_LEARN
parameters.description: Enable or disable the automatic learning of compass offsets. You can enable learning either using a compass-only method that is suitable only for fixed wing aircraft or using the offsets learnt by the active EKF state estimator. If this option is enabled then the learnt offsets are saved when you disarm the vehicle. If InFlight learning is enabled then the compass with automatically start learning once a flight starts (must be armed). While InFlight learning is running you cannot use position control modes.

parameters.name: COMPASS_MOT2_X
parameters.display_name: Motor interference compensation to compass2 for body frame X axis
parameters.description: Multiplied by the current throttle and added to compass2's x-axis values to compensate for motor interference (Offset per Amp or at Full Throttle)

parameters.name: COMPASS_MOT2_Y
parameters.display_name: Motor interference compensation to compass2 for body frame Y axis
parameters.description: Multiplied by the current throttle and added to compass2's y-axis values to compensate for motor interference (Offset per Amp or at Full Throttle)

parameters.name: COMPASS_MOT2_Z
parameters.display_name: Motor interference compensation to compass2 for body frame Z axis
parameters.description: Multiplied by the current throttle and added to compass2's z-axis values to compensate for motor interference (Offset per Amp or at Full Throttle)

parameters.name: COMPASS_MOT3_X
parameters.display_name: Motor interference compensation to compass3 for body frame X axis
parameters.description: Multiplied by the current throttle and added to compass3's x-axis values to compensate for motor interference (Offset per Amp or at Full Throttle)

parameters.name: COMPASS_MOT3_Y
parameters.display_name: Motor interference compensation to compass3 for body frame Y axis
parameters.description: Multiplied by the current throttle and added to compass3's y-axis values to compensate for motor interference (Offset per Amp or at Full Throttle)

parameters.name: COMPASS_MOT3_Z
parameters.display_name: Motor interference compensation to compass3 for body frame Z axis
parameters.description: Multiplied by the current throttle and added to compass3's z-axis values to compensate for motor interference (Offset per Amp or at Full Throttle)

parameters.name: COMPASS_MOTCT
parameters.description: Set motor interference compensation type to disabled, throttle or current.  Do not change manually.

parameters.name: COMPASS_MOT_X
parameters.display_name: Motor interference compensation for body frame X axis
parameters.description: Multiplied by the current throttle and added to the compass's x-axis values to compensate for motor interference (Offset per Amp or at Full Throttle)

parameters.name: COMPASS_MOT_Y
parameters.display_name: Motor interference compensation for body frame Y axis
parameters.description: Multiplied by the current throttle and added to the compass's y-axis values to compensate for motor interference (Offset per Amp or at Full Throttle)

parameters.name: COMPASS_MOT_Z
parameters.display_name: Motor interference compensation for body frame Z axis
parameters.description: Multiplied by the current throttle and added to the compass's z-axis values to compensate for motor interference (Offset per Amp or at Full Throttle)

parameters.description: ODI_X in the compass2 soft-iron calibration matrix: [[DIA_X, ODI_X, ODI_Y], [ODI_X, DIA_Y, ODI_Z], [ODI_Y, ODI_Z, DIA_Z]]

parameters.name: COMPASS_ODI3_Z
parameters.description: ODI_Z in the compass3 soft-iron calibration matrix: [[DIA_X, ODI_X, ODI_Y], [ODI_X, DIA_Y, ODI_Z], [ODI_Y, ODI_Z, DIA_Z]]

parameters.name: COMPASS_ODI_Y

parameters.description: This sets the maximum allowed compass offset in calibration and arming checks

parameters.description: Offset to be added to compass2's x-axis values to compensate for metal in the frame

parameters.name: COMPASS_OFS2_Y

parameters.description: Offset to be added to compass3's y-axis values to compensate for metal in the frame

parameters.name: COMPASS_OFS3_Z
parameters.display_name: Compass3 offsets in milligauss on the Z axis
parameters.description: Offset to be added to compass3's z-axis values to compensate for metal in the frame

parameters.name: COMPASS_OFS_X
parameters.display_name: Compass offsets in milligauss on the X axis
parameters.description: Offset to be added to the compass x-axis values to compensate for metal in the frame

parameters.name: COMPASS_OFS_Y
parameters.display_name: Compass offsets in milligauss on the Y axis
parameters.description: Offset to be added to the compass y-axis values to compensate for metal in the frame

parameters.name: COMPASS_OPTIONS
parameters.description: This sets options to change the behaviour of the compass

bitmask.1: Allow missing DroneCAN compasses to be automaticaly replaced (calibration still required)

parameters.name: COMPASS_ORIENT
parameters.description: The orientation of the first external compass relative to the vehicle frame. This value will be ignored unless this compass is set as an external compass. When set correctly in the northern hemisphere, pointing the nose and right side down should increase the MagX and MagY values respectively. Rolling the vehicle upside down should decrease the MagZ value. For southern hemisphere, switch increase and decrease. NOTE: For internal compasses, AHRS_ORIENT is used. The label for each option is specified in the order of rotations for that orientation. Firmware versions 4.2 and prior can use a CUSTOM (100) rotation to set the COMPASS_CUS_ROLL/PIT/YAW angles for Compass orientation. Later versions provide two general custom rotations which can be used, Custom 1 and Custom 2, with CUST_1_ROLL/PIT/YAW or CUST_2_ROLL/PIT/YAW angles.

parameters.name: COMPASS_ORIENT2
parameters.description: The orientation of a second external compass relative to the vehicle frame. This value will be ignored unless this compass is set as an external compass. When set correctly in the northern hemisphere, pointing the nose and right side down should increase the MagX and MagY values respectively. Rolling the vehicle upside down should decrease the MagZ value. For southern hemisphere, switch increase and decrease. NOTE: For internal compasses, AHRS_ORIENT is used. The label for each option is specified in the order of rotations for that orientation. Firmware versions 4.2 and prior can use a CUSTOM (100) rotation to set the COMPASS_CUS_ROLL/PIT/YAW angles for Compass orientation. Later versions provide two general custom rotations which can be used, Custom 1 and Custom 2, with CUST_1_ROLL/PIT/YAW or CUST_2_ROLL/PIT/YAW angles.

parameters.name: COMPASS_ORIENT3
parameters.description: The orientation of a third external compass relative to the vehicle frame. This value will be ignored unless this compass is set as an external compass. When set correctly in the northern hemisphere, pointing the nose and right side down should increase the MagX and MagY values respectively. Rolling the vehicle upside down should decrease the MagZ value. For southern hemisphere, switch increase and decrease. NOTE: For internal compasses, AHRS_ORIENT is used. The label for each option is specified in the order of rotations for that orientation. Firmware versions 4.2 and prior can use a CUSTOM (100) rotation to set the COMPASS_CUS_ROLL/PIT/YAW angles for Compass orientation. Later versions provide two general custom rotations which can be used, Custom 1 and Custom 2, with CUST_1_ROLL/PIT/YAW or CUST_2_ROLL/PIT/YAW angles.

parameters.description: Compensation for X axis of motor1

parameters.description: Compensation for Y axis of motor1

parameters.description: Compensation for Y axis of motor4

parameters.name: COMPASS_PMOT4_Z
parameters.description: Compensation for Z axis of motor4

parameters.name: COMPASS_PMOT_EN
parameters.description: This enables per-motor compass corrections

parameters.name: COMPASS_PMOT_EXP
parameters.description: This is the exponential correction for the power output of the motor for per-motor compass correction

parameters.name: COMPASS_PRIO1_ID
parameters.display_name: Compass device id with 1st order priority
parameters.description: Compass device id with 1st order priority, set automatically if 0. Reboot required after change.

parameters.name: COMPASS_PRIO2_ID
parameters.display_name: Compass device id with 2nd order priority
parameters.description: Compass device id with 2nd order priority, set automatically if 0. Reboot required after change.

parameters.name: COMPASS_PRIO3_ID
parameters.display_name: Compass device id with 3rd order priority
parameters.description: Compass device id with 3rd order priority, set automatically if 0. Reboot required after change.

parameters.name: COMPASS_SCALE
parameters.description: Scaling factor for first compass to compensate for sensor scaling errors. If this is 0 then no scaling is done

parameters.name: COMPASS_SCALE2
parameters.description: Scaling factor for 2nd compass to compensate for sensor scaling errors. If this is 0 then no scaling is done

parameters.name: COMPASS_SCALE3
parameters.description: Scaling factor for 3rd compass to compensate for sensor scaling errors. If this is 0 then no scaling is done

parameters.name: COMPASS_USE
parameters.description: Enable or disable the use of the compass (instead of the GPS) for determining heading

parameters.name: COMPASS_USE2
parameters.description: Enable or disable the secondary compass for determining heading.

parameters.name: COMPASS_USE3
parameters.description: Enable or disable the tertiary compass for determining heading.

parameters.name: EK3_LOG_LEVEL
parameters.description: Determines how verbose the EKF3 streaming logging is. A value of 0 provides full logging(default), a value of 1 only XKF4 scaled innovations are logged, a value of 2 both XKF4 and GSF are logged, and a value of 3 disables all streaming logging of EKF3.

parameters.name: FS_*
parameters.description: Wildcard metadata for FS_ parameter family; exact meaning depends on the specific parameter.

parameters.name: FS_CRASH_CHECK
parameters.description: This enables automatic crash checking. When enabled the motors will disarm if a crash is detected.

parameters.name: FS_DR_ENABLE
parameters.description: Failsafe action taken immediately as deadreckoning starts. Deadreckoning starts when EKF loses position and velocity source and relies only on wind estimates

parameters.name: FS_DR_TIMEOUT
parameters.description: DeadReckoning is available for this many seconds after losing position and/or velocity source.  After this timeout elapses the EKF failsafe will trigger in modes requiring a position estimate

parameters.name: FS_EKF_ACTION
parameters.description: Controls the action that will be taken when an EKF failsafe is invoked

values.1: Switch to Land mode if current mode requires position
values.2: Switch to AltHold mode if current mode requires position
values.3: Switch to Land mode from all modes

parameters.name: FS_EKF_FILT
parameters.description: EKF Failsafe filter cutoff frequency. EKF variances are filtered using this value to avoid spurious failsafes from transient high variances. A higher value means the failsafe is more likely to trigger.

parameters.name: FS_EKF_THRESH
parameters.description: Allows setting the maximum acceptable compass, velocity, position and height variances. Used in arming check and EKF failsafe.

parameters.name: FS_GCS_ENABLE
parameters.description: Controls whether failsafe will be invoked (and what action to take) when connection with Ground station is lost for at least 5 seconds. See FS_OPTIONS param for additional actions, or for cases allowing Mission continuation, when GCS failsafe is enabled.

values.2: RTL or Continue with Mission in Auto Mode (Removed in 4.0+-see FS_OPTIONS)

parameters.name: FS_GCS_TIMEOUT
parameters.description: Timeout before triggering the GCS failsafe

parameters.name: FS_OPTIONS
parameters.description: Bitmask of additional options for battery, radio, & GCS failsafes. 0 disables all options.

bitmask.0: Continue if in Auto on RC failsafe
bitmask.1: Continue if in Auto on GCS failsafe
bitmask.2: Continue if in Guided on RC failsafe
bitmask.3: Continue if landing on any failsafe
bitmask.4: Continue if in pilot controlled modes on GCS failsafe

parameters.name: FS_THR_ENABLE
parameters.description: The throttle failsafe allows you to configure a software failsafe activated by a setting on the throttle input channel

values.2: Enabled Continue with Mission in Auto Mode (Removed in 4.0+)

parameters.name: FS_THR_VALUE
parameters.description: The PWM level in microseconds on channel 3 below which throttle failsafe triggers

parameters.name: FS_VIBE_ENABLE
parameters.description: This enables the vibration failsafe which will use modified altitude estimation and control during high vibrations

parameters.name: INS_LOG_BAT_MASK
parameters.description: Bitmap of which IMUs to log batch data for. This option takes effect on the next reboot.

parameters.name: INS_LOG_BAT_OPT
parameters.description: Options for the BatchSampler.

bitmask.0: Sensor-Rate Logging (sample at full sensor rate seen by AP)

parameters.name: INS_RAW_LOG_OPT
parameters.description: Raw logging options bitmask

parameters.name: LOG_BACKEND_TYPE
parameters.description: Bitmap of what Logger backend types to enable. Block-based logging is available on SITL and boards with dataflash chips. Multiple backends can be selected.

parameters.name: LOG_BITMASK
parameters.description: Bitmap of what on-board log types to enable. This value is made up of the sum of each of the log types you want to be saved. It is usually best just to enable all basiclog types by setting this to 65535. Note that if you want to reduce log sizes you should consider using LOG_FILE_RATEMAX instead of disabling logging items with this parameter.

parameters.name: LOG_BLK_RATEMAX
parameters.display_name: Maximum logging rate for block backend
parameters.description: This sets the maximum rate that streaming log messages will be logged to the block backend. A value of zero means that rate limiting is disabled.

parameters.name: LOG_DARM_RATEMAX
parameters.description: This sets the maximum rate that streaming log messages will be logged to any backend when disarmed. A value of zero means that the normal backend rate limit is applied.

parameters.name: LOG_DISARMED
parameters.description: If LOG_DISARMED is set to 1 then logging will be enabled at all times including when disarmed. Logging before arming can make for very large logfiles but can help a lot when tracking down startup issues and is necessary if logging of EKF replay data is selected via the LOG_REPLAY parameter. If LOG_DISARMED is set to 2, then logging will be enabled when disarmed, but not if a USB connection is detected. This can be used to prevent unwanted data logs being generated when the vehicle is connected via USB for log downloading or parameter changes. If LOG_DISARMED is set to 3 then logging will happen while disarmed, but if the vehicle never arms then the logs using the filesystem backend will be discarded on the next boot.

values.3: Discard log on reboot if never armed

parameters.name: LOG_FILE_RATEMAX
parameters.display_name: Maximum logging rate for file backend
parameters.description: This sets the maximum rate that streaming log messages will be logged to the file backend. A value of zero means that rate limiting is disabled.

parameters.name: MOT_YAW_HEADROOM
parameters.description: Yaw control is given at least this pwm in microseconds range

parameters.name: RCMAP_*
parameters.description: Wildcard metadata for RCMAP_ parameter family; exact meaning depends on the specific parameter.

parameters.name: RCMAP_PITCH
parameters.description: Pitch channel number. This is useful when you have a RC transmitter that can't change the channel order easily. Pitch is normally on channel 2, but you can move it to any channel with this parameter.  Reboot is required for changes to take effect.

parameters.name: RCMAP_ROLL
parameters.description: Roll channel number. This is useful when you have a RC transmitter that can't change the channel order easily. Roll is normally on channel 1, but you can move it to any channel with this parameter.  Reboot is required for changes to take effect.

parameters.name: RCMAP_THROTTLE
parameters.description: Throttle channel number. This is useful when you have a RC transmitter that can't change the channel order easily. Throttle is normally on channel 3, but you can move it to any channel with this parameter. Reboot is required for changes to take effect.

parameters.name: RCMAP_YAW
parameters.description: Yaw channel number. This is useful when you have a RC transmitter that can't change the channel order easily. Yaw (also known as rudder) is normally on channel 4, but you can move it to any channel with this parameter.  Reboot is required for changes to take effect.

parameters.name: RC_OPTIONS
parameters.description: RC input options

bitmask.11: Use Link Quality for RSSI with CRSF
bitmask.12: Annotate CRSF flight mode with * on disarm
bitmask.2: Ignore Receiver Failsafe bit but allow other RC failsafes if setup
bitmask.6: Skip the arming check for neutral Roll/Pitch/Yaw sticks
bitmask.9: Suppress CRSF mode/rate message for ELRS systems

parameters.name: SERVO*_FUNCTION
parameters.description: Wildcard metadata for SERVOx_FUNCTION output assignments.

parameters.name: SERVO1_FUNCTION
parameters.description: Function assigned to this servo. Setting this to Disabled(0) will setup this output for control by auto missions or MAVLink servo set commands. any other value will enable the corresponding function

parameters.name: WP_YAW_BEHAVIOR
parameters.description: Determines how the autopilot controls the yaw during missions and RTL
